name: Grid interconnection queues
severityNote: PJM 5-7 yr; structural across all ISOs
description: Grid interconnection queues are the wall every new datacenter must scale to get connected to the transmission system. PJM's queue alone holds >250 GW of pending generation and load requests, with study cycles measured in years. The constraint is not a supplier market — it is a regulatory and engineering process inside the ISOs/RTOs.
stackPosition: Site selection (existing brownfield with capacity headroom > greenfield with full queue cycle) → interconnection request → feasibility study → system impact study → facilities study → interconnection agreement → network upgrade construction → energization. Each ISO runs the cycle differently; PJM/MISO are the slowest, ERCOT (which is not FERC-jurisdictional) is structurally faster. Co-located behind-the-meter generation can short-circuit the queue but creates its own regulatory friction.

supplyStructure.name: ERCOT
supplyStructure.note: Fastest queue; non-FERC; reliability margins tightening

indicators.name: PJM queue reports — new requests, completed studies, withdrawals
MISO, ERCOT, CAISO queue dashboards

indicators.name: FERC orders on interconnection process (e.g. Order 2023 implementation)
State PUC rulings on co-located load + behind-the-meter generation
indicators.source: PUC dockets — PA, VA, OH, TX

indicators.name: LBNL Berkeley Lab queue and interconnection analysis
Hyperscaler PPA + greenfield datacenter siting announcements

companies.name: Vistra
companies.note: Existing nuclear + gas fleet with capacity to pair with data load (Comanche Peak / Perry)

companies.name: Constellation Energy
companies.note: Nuclear fleet behind Microsoft / TMI restart thesis

companies.name: Talen Energy
companies.note: Susquehanna nuclear PPA with Amazon — front-running the model

companies.name: NextEra Energy
companies.note: Largest renewables developer; many projects stuck in queues

companies.name: Bloom Energy
companies.note: Solid-oxide fuel cells — fast deploy, behind-the-meter alternative

companies.name: Cummins
companies.note: Reciprocating engines + diesel/gas gensets for stop-gap power

companies.name: Last Energy
companies.note: 20 MW micro-modular reactors; targeting datacenters with PPA-first model

companies.name: X-Energy
companies.note: Xe-100 HTGR SMR; Amazon $500M strategic investment for AWS datacenter power
companies.investors: Amazon, Ares Management, Citadel, Ken Griffin

companies.name: Fervo Energy
companies.note: Enhanced geothermal; live Google PPA in Nevada, scaling Utah project

companies.name: TerraPower
companies.note: Natrium fast reactor; Wyoming pilot site, Gates-backed

insight: Interconnection queues are the most binding non-OEM constraint in the AI capex chain — every announced datacenter MW has to scale this wall, and the wall is not getting materially shorter for new requests. The trade is anyone with *existing* energization: nuclear operators (Vistra, Constellation, Talen) with co-locatable capacity, and behind-the-meter rapid-deploy generation (Bloom, Cummins) that bypasses the queue entirely. The widow-maker is greenfield renewables developers whose pipelines are queued behind data-load fast-tracks — even good projects get pushed to 2030+. Watch FERC Order 2023 cluster-study implementation: a credible reform path could re-rate the renewables developer pipeline.

debates.question: Does PJM new-load queue time drop below 4 years by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Cluster-study reform helps at the margin; underlying transmission build-out still dominates the timeline

debates.question: Do nuclear restarts (TMI, Palisades, Duane Arnold) reach commercial operation by 2028?
debates.currentBelief: TMI (Crane) and Palisades on track; Duane Arnold uncertain. PPA economics drive the decisions

debates.question: Does FERC mandate uniform interconnection reform across all ISOs by end of 2026?
debates.currentBelief: Partial — Order 2023 implementation is in flight, but ERCOT remains structurally separate
